questions.text: Organisms that prepare their own food are called:
questions.explanation: Auto means self and trophos means nourishment; hence plants are autotrophs.

questions.text: The green pigment present in leaves is called:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll helps leaves capture the energy of sunlight for photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which gas is taken in by plants during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide from the air through stomata for food synthesis.

questions.text: Cuscuta (Amarbel) is an example of a:
questions.explanation: Cuscuta does not have chlorophyll and takes ready-made food from the host plant.

questions.text: The tiny pores on the surface of leaves are called:
questions.explanation: Stomata are surrounded by guard cells and facilitate gas exchange.

questions.text: Which of these is an insectivorous plant?
questions.explanation: Pitcher plants trap and digest insects to fulfill their nitrogen requirements.

questions.text: The mode of nutrition in which organisms take in nutrients from dead and decaying matter is:
questions.explanation: Fungi are common examples of saprotrophs that live on decaying matter.

questions.text: Lichens represent a symbiotic relationship between:
questions.explanation: In lichens, the fungus provides shelter/water, and the alga prepares food.

questions.text: Which bacterium helps in atmospheric nitrogen fixation in leguminous plants?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium lives in root nodules and converts atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form.

questions.text: The ultimate source of energy for all living organisms is:
questions.explanation: Sunlight is the primary source of energy that plants convert into chemical energy.
